issue_title: Custom theme - url.resourcesCommonPath references wrong theme
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

In a custom theme with own `template.ftl` it is not possible to reference resources (in this case CSS) in the parent theme it is using. The theme in question references "keycloak in theme.properties:

```
parent=keycloak
```

Since the update from 20.0.2 to 21.1.1 the references inside the ftl are forced to use the active theme

| 20.0.2 | 21.1.1 |
| ------ | ------ |
| ![20 0 2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6563013/235657432-3d528d6f-cdfd-4262-b4b5-6d2e5fa4384d.png) | ![21 1 1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6563013/235657449-dde72423-d5d3-4768-912c-7df6fcee3506.png) |

See how the patternfly css files are correctly retrieved in the "keycloak" theme in 20.0.2, but are retrieved from "nx-swl" in 21.1.1. In the latter they don't exist, as they are simply inherited from the "keycloak" theme.

The `template.ftl` gets those stylesheets using the following code
```
<link rel="icon" href="${url.resourcesPath}/img/favicon.ico" />
<#if properties.stylesCommon?has_content>
	<#list properties.stylesCommon?split(' ') as style>
		<link href="${url.resourcesCommonPath}/${style}" rel="stylesheet" />
	</#list>
</#if>
```


### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Retrieve those stylesheets the way 20.0.2 did (referencing the theme where they are exist)

### Actual behavior

Force files to be included in the custom theme, rather than using those from the theme they are provided.

### How to Reproduce?

Perform steps from https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_development/#_themes
The theme used to reproduce this locally is a login theme only if this matters.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Custom theme - url.resourcesCommonPath references wrong theme
closes #20085

issue_title: Support for non-XA databases
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Looks like we are no longer supporting the `RESOURCE_LOCAL` transaction type in the default PU but forcing JTA. See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/17664/commits/05417c48b82d5cd2b85b8803578e624c12260efc.

Not sure about the motivation behind the change above but some databases do not work well if not disabling JTA completely.

We also have tests to cover those use cases. For that, see `org.keycloak.it.storage.database.MSSQLTest`. Although this test is out-dated and not run in our CI, we need to make sure the changes herein linked are not going to introduce backward compatibility issues.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

Make it possible to use databases that does not support XA.

### Actual behavior

Failing to run using a database that does not support XA.

### How to Reproduce?

Run the `org.keycloak.it.storage.database.MSSQLTest`.

### Anything else?

Possible related issues:

* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16192
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12379
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12061
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/10339
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/10191
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/9133

issue_title: Role mapping tab no longer visible when using fine grained permissions after upgrade from 20.0.3 to 21.0.2
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

We have been using fine grained permissions with Keycloak [20.0.3](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/20.0.3).

A user using fine grained permissions was able to view the `Role mapping` tab when navigating a `Group` found in `Groups` in the admin UI.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/171731/235934840-3c20aada-dfa4-4988-b42a-85c370eb6195.png)

The issue: this tab "disappeared" after upgrading Keycloak to version [21.0.2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/21.0.2).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/171731/235935150-c816e64f-bc51-457f-951c-80d57f55282c.png)

Note:
* No Keycloak configuration or permissions changes had been made meanwhile
* We also tested to see whether upgrading to version [21.1.1](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/21.1.1) would resolve it, which it unfortunately did not


### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

A user using fine grained permissions should be able to view the `Role mapping` tab when navigating a `Group` found in `Groups` in the admin UI.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/171731/235934840-3c20aada-dfa4-4988-b42a-85c370eb6195.png)


### Actual behavior

A user using fine grained permissions is unable to view the `Role mapping` tab when navigating a `Group` found in `Groups` in the admin UI.

This tab "disappeared" after upgrading Keycloak to version [21.0.2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/21.0.2).


### How to Reproduce?

- in a Keycloak instance version `20.0.3`
- setup basic fine grained permissions for a specific user with a groups "manage" role linked to a policy granting that specific user these permissions
- verify that the user is able to see for a group in groups, the `Role mapping` tab
- upgrade the Keycloak instance to version `21.0.2`

expected:
- the user is able to see for a group in groups, the `Role mapping` tab 

actual:
- the user does not longer see the `Role mapping` tab


### Anything else?

_No response_

Make role mapping tab visible with view user permissions (#26386)

issue_title: NPE after import validation in UserStorageManager::getUserByEmail
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

If an import validation returns null, indicating that the user entity should be removed from local storage, during UserStorageManager::getUserByEmail a NullPointerException is raised due to an email equality check is performed without checking if the returned `UserModel` is null.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

To not get a NullPointerException and that the federated user is removed from local storage.

### Actual behavior

A NullPointerException is thrown.

### How to Reproduce?

Add a custom user federation provider that implements `ImportedUserValidation` which always returns null:
```java
public UserModel validate(RealmModel realmModel, UserModel userModel) {
    return null;
}
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fixes a NullPointerException after import validation (#20151)

* Fixes a NullPointerException after import validation

If the import validation (when getting a user by email)
returns null, indicating that the user entity should be
removed from local storage, an email equality check results
in a NullPointerException.

This commit fixes this issue by explicitly checking for null.

Closes #20150

---------
Co-authored-by: Michal Hajas <mhajas@redhat.com>

issue_title: User defined message bundles do not apply correctly to Admin Console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When applying message bundles defined by the user in the realm settings these do not show up properly in the Admin Console.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

When defining a message bundle with the key `foo:bar.baz` and value `Hello World` this should be merged into the localization of the Admin Console under the namespace `foo` with the following structure:

```js
{
  bar: {
    baz: "Hello World"
  }
}
```

### Actual behavior

The message bundle is applied flatly, meaning that `bar.baz` ends up as the key without transforming it into a nested object. This means that messages such as the [option labels](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e50580c46501f3269d74f567189472a34a9fcdf4/js/apps/admin-ui/public/locales/en/clients.json#L2-L6) of the protocol type of a client cannot be localized.

Similarly namespaces are not taken into account, so if a label is defined with the key `foo` it will replace all labels in **every** namespace with the same key name. This might cause unexpected behavior when namespaces expect different values for a similar key (e.g. "Create user" and "Create client" could share the same `createLabel` key in their respective namepaces).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable localization under realm settings
2. Add a new message to the bundle with a key that overrides an existing label.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Rework merging of message bundles for localization of Admin Console (#20183)

Closes #20182

commit_message:
Fix pagination issue with H6

With Hibernate ORM 6, pagination started to be unreliable: When
setting the max results only if the first row was 0 has randomly
affected other threads where first row was greater than 0. The
latter thread sometimes produced query which did *not* account
for the offset (cf. threads `-t1` and `-t2` below, while `-t2`
missed the `offset ? rows` part whic `-t3` has).

This has been fixed by setting the first row offset unconditionally.

Closes: #20202
Closes: #16570

issue_title: Cannot retrieve initial access token if created with kcadm
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

calling `./kcadm.sh create clients-initial-access -r master -s count=5  -s expiration=60 -o` will create an initial access token but kcamd does not print the token

This issues was also mentioned [here](https://lists.jboss.org/pipermail/keycloak-user/2019-January/016977.html) and curl was suggested as a workaround.

The issue arises because `-o` triggers an additional get, but the token can only be retrieved from the initial json response:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/1d2a98d747372f909e330edc3e85e32de92fbfc9/integration/client-cli/admin-cli/src/main/java/org/keycloak/client/admin/cli/commands/AbstractRequestCmd.java#L385:L400 

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

return the json object like

`{"id":"4913c63e-6ed4-48cd-b1af-cd9091685494","token":"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICJmNmI3ODhmMi0yNDNiLTQ3NGQtYjY3OC00NGFlM2Y5OTExZTkifQ.eyJleHAiOjE2ODM1NTQ3MzMsImlhdCI6MTY4MzU1NDY3MywianRpIjoiNDkxM2M2M2UtNmVkNC00OGNkLWIxYWYtY2Q5MDkxNjg1NDk0IiwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cDovL2xvY2FsaG9zdDo4MDgwL3JlYWxtcy9tYXN0ZXIiLCJhdWQiOiJodHRwOi8vbG9jYWxob3N0OjgwODAvcmVhbG1zL21hc3RlciIsInR5cCI6IkluaXRpYWxBY2Nlc3NUb2tlbiJ9.KOnnjyy1EBa6T2HUOeG_FudrNnr8iXX2WsjKWloBDGo","timestamp":1683554673,"expiration":60,"count":1,"remainingCount":1}`



### Actual behavior

The token id gets returned

`Created new clients-initial-acces with id '4913c63e-6ed4-48cd-b1af-cd9091685494'`

### How to Reproduce?

1. `kcadm.sh config credentials --server http://localhost:8080 --realm master --user admin --password $PASSWORD`
2. `kcadm.sh create clients-initial-access -r master -s count=5 -s expiration=60 -c -o`

### Anything else?

issue_title: Role mappers must return a single value when they are not multivalued
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Both built-in realm and client role mappers are not properly handling the `multivalued` attribute. Instead, `string`-typed claims are always set using a stringified JSON array.

It is also not behaving similarly to other mappers that also allow configuring single/multi-valued claims.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

When setting the `multivalued` to `false`, the claim value should be a single string. If based on a list, only the first element is considered.

### Actual behavior

When setting the `multivalued` to `false`, the claim is still represented as an array when the values come from a list.

### How to Reproduce?

* Change either the role or client role mappers to use single-valued claims (`multivalued==false`)
* Issue an access token for a user with multiple roles and note that a stringified JSON array is set to the claim

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Adhere to HTML standard when using `ul`-element
issue_body:
### Description

According to the HTML specification, the `<ul>`-element only allows `<li>`-elements as content:

https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#the-ul-element

In the base-theme, the HTML spec is violated by putting `<a>`-tags directly inside a `<ul>`-tag:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/025778fe9c745316f80b53fe3052aeb314e868ef/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login.ftl#L88-L100

The HTML renders fine (because of browser parsing magic), but it should be corrected.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

To not confuse screen-readers or other HTML parsers and provide good accessibility, it is important to use semantic (correct) HTML.

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/Accessibility/Accessibility_troubleshooting#semantic_html

### Details

A fix exists in #20243.
commit_message:
Adhere to HTML standard when using ul-element (#20243)

Closes #20250

commit_message:
Fix CacheExpirationTest

Sometimes the initialization of keycloak session factory
took longer than the expiration was set on the elements,
so they were not found in the cache where they were awaited.
This is fixed by adding an assumption on the time, and if the
time is over the expiration, the remainder of the test is skipped.
The timeout is set in order to run fully most of the time in GHA.

Closes: #20269

```js
const kc = new Keycloak({
    realm: 'my_realm',
    clientId: 'my_client',
```

issue_title: Deleting a client scope in the Admin UI should redirect to the list of ClientScopes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When deleting a client scope in the Admin UI from the client scope details view, the browser doesn't return to the list of client scopes. 
Instead, it stays on that page which indicates that the client scope hasn't been deleted as expected. Deleting it again produces an error message as the client scope has already been deleted. 

The behavior works as expected with other entities like for example clients

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

When deleting a client scope in the details view should show the list of remaining client scopes in the browser once the action succeeds.

### Actual behavior

When deleting a client scope in the details view with the action on the upper right corner, the client details remain open. Trying to delete it again produces an error message as the client scope has already been deleted. 

### How to Reproduce?

* Open the admin console
* Choose client scopes
* Create a new client scope. This will open the details of the newly created client scope
* Choose the Action *Delete* in the upper right corner
* Confirm the deletion. Then the browser still shows the client scope details page and the action to delete it can be triggered again

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/3957921/aec7f420-420f-4135-9abd-4a23bd40e56d)


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Double form submit in Admin UI possible leading to error mesages
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When pressing the save button multiple times "double clicking", or if the response takes a longer time, the UI will submit the form contents again. 

This leads to submitting the same information again. When this is a create request, the second create will fail when there is for example as with clients a unique key on the clientId. 

This leads to error like the following: 

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/3957921/55af6409-e936-4382-b12e-5f1b81eb5a86)


### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

Submitting a form should be possible only once when a second submission would lead to an error. If the submission is processing, the "Save" button might change its color, show a spinner or something similar. 

In general, it might be good to have a feedback on all forms that it is currently processing. Other applications add a "glass pane" and editing form contents is not possible while the submission is in progress. 



### Actual behavior

* The form can be submitted multiple times, and all except the first one lead to an error.
* The form data can be changed after the data has been submitted and before the response is being returned.

### How to Reproduce?

* In the developer tool, switch to "Slow 3G throttling"
* Create a new client. On the "Save" button, click "Save" multiple times before the next page appears
* Watch different toast message appear; one in green, others in red.

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

In a recent PR #19839, NPM was replaced with PNPM. issue_title: ClientScope changes don't invalidate the realm cache
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When changing the ClientScopes are modified via `session.clientScopes()`, the list of client scopes which is cached as part of the realm is not evicted. 

Due to that, an access in the next request will use the cached version that doesn't contain the updated list (for example an added clientScope).

This doesn't happen when using the UI to add a ClientScope as those go through `realm().addClientScope()`, which will expire the realm cache when fetching the delegate

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

The cached realm contents should expire.

### Actual behavior

The cached realm is not expired.

### How to Reproduce?

I will provide a model test case as part of a pull request.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Administration / Keycloak Admin REST API documentation can no longer be generated
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

This is specific to the API docs generated from the `services` module and there are a couple of related problems.

1. Due to the switch to `jakarta.*` from `javax.*` for the JAX-RS annotations, the Admin API documentation can no longer be generated by the `swagger-doclet` declared in the `services/pom.xml`.
2. The services repo now needs to be compiled with Java 11+. However this is problematic as the `swagger-doclet` also has a hard dependency upon the `tools.jar` that was shipped in JDK 8 and below. 
3. There is a temporary solution for item 2, but that solution doesn't work on JDK 17.

Issue 1 would require a change in the `swagger-doclet` to process the `jakarta.*` based annotations. This is unlikely to happen outside of a fork.

Issue 2 can be bypassed by copying the JAR from an OpenJDK 8 install to the similar location in JDK 11, however this is not ideal and doesn't work with JDK 17+ due to other issues.

### Version

main / latest

### Expected behavior

API documentation is generated as expected

### Actual behavior

API documentation is not generated.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Have JDK 11 with tools.jar in the lib directory (basically whatever `<systemPath>${java.home}/../lib/tools.jar</systemPath>` resolves to)
2) clone Keycloak Repository
3) `cd services && mvn -s ../maven-settings.xml -Pjboss-release clean package`
4) Look in `services/target/apidocs-rest/output/index.html` and see that there is no API documentation generated.

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/4944041/53f60202-f134-46d0-bff0-f252d14c1c39)


To test the positive case when it was working, checkout the `21.1.1` tag, use JDK 8 and run step 3 and 4 from above.

### Anything else?

The primary artifact responsible for generating this documentation does not appear to be maintained very well, and may prove to an interesting challenge to replace (fork? redo this documentation entirely?)

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/c30b234bbb163c82dbd4a4270f31800e5a4596f3/services/pom.xml#L257-L261

Comment about the swagger-doclet no longer being maintained / forked
https://github.com/teamcarma/swagger-jaxrs-doclet/commit/ec505a4070fcf0d6cbaa740c92a1a878aeebffd4
commit_message:
Refactor Admin REST API Documentation to use OpenAPI annotations.

Removes dependencies on swagger-doclet
Adds dependencies on microprofile-openapi-api
Plugins for smallrye-open-api-maven-plugin, openapi-generator-maven-plugin

Customized ascii doc template for openapi-generator-maven-plugin, to give similar feel to previous documentation.

OpenAPI annotations added to Admin REST API resources.

Closes keycloak/keycloak#20433

issue_title: Permission Evaluation Issue on custom attribute evaluation on RPT token generation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

I am trying to create regex-based policy in client authorization with the attribute that is created in the group. And mapped the policy to the scope-based-permission and evaluated in the Keycloak itself and it is working fine and getting the scope properly. But when I get the RPT token in the authorization's permissions that scope is not found. When we checked in the current version(21.1.1) also the same issue is present. 

On analyzed the issue we found that it is because the claim structure we are trying to evaluate is having the structure 'claimname.value'. If I configure to get the value to be present is 'claimname' itself and configured the policy to evaluate it is working as expected. So, we assume that the Evaluation engine is not given support for that kind structure during the RPT generation.

The behavior is as follows:
1. Created Attribute in the group called 'ADMIN' as follows
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/4189244e-9113-49ca-afe3-08ee237842bf)

2. Added 'admin; user to the above group
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/7c48cc19-d1d1-4bae-bd34-f2351e0a876b)

3. Created 'Client Scopes' and mapper to get the group attribute in the JWT token at realm level and marked as default
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/0c272f81-23f4-4c8c-9218-8db665f5b24e)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/20ae3ebd-2ac9-4147-820e-edad1a831a0d)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/885fe07f-aedd-4429-a0f7-ef777eced291)

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/d81e7a6b-97a7-420c-bd83-0b9328236d53)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/182218b4-8a17-4b7f-906b-d35982c16368)

5. Mapper this in the scope-based permission to get the 'read' scope
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/b272cd67-42a8-4cd3-ae16-fa844df4446c)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/e0f197d9-a96f-4e90-b80b-5be19400b5a5)

6. When I use the evaluation option in the keycloak it is giving the permission for that scope
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/079e2373-f8fa-4119-8ae7-bb57ff46747c)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/152a4a96-667a-4a5f-af24-a4670f541ec0)

7. when I am the RPT token using the token API POST: http://localhost:8080/realms/dev/protocol/openid-connect/token by passing the authentication token with the following 2 values grant_type = urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket, audience = service-client in the token the 'read' scope is missing for the same user. Even the token is having the mapper value
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/1dae0cfa-cf4f-4384-9df5-bbec0f3079fa)

8. This is behavior is working fine when I change the 'Token Claim Name' in the Mapper to 'attributes' from 'attributes.values' and changed the policy accordingly.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

The Evaluation must behave properly as per the Regex configured in the policy based on the claim structure.

### Actual behavior

If the claim structure is represented as Object1.Object2=value is failing for the regex written for the structure Object1.Object2 even with the exact match.

### How to Reproduce?

The behavior is as follows:
1. Created Attribute in the group called 'ADMIN' as follows
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/4189244e-9113-49ca-afe3-08ee237842bf)

2. Added 'admin; user to the above group
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/7c48cc19-d1d1-4bae-bd34-f2351e0a876b)

3. Created 'Client Scopes' and mapper to get the group attribute in the JWT token at realm level and marked as default

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/75622411/20ae3ebd-2ac9-4147-820e-edad1a831a0d)

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

In case of LDAP User Federation if there is an backend error while fetching users, response returned by Keycloak is not a valid JSON.  Response from the server is : 

[]{"error":"unknown_error"}

Depending on the setup array can sometimes contains user objects.

21.1.1

url: https://localhost:8086/auth/admin/realms/myrealm/users?briefRepresentation=true&first=0&max=20&search=*

issue_title: Changing the email address has no impact at username regardless "Email as username" toggle
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

Changing the email address ha no impact at username regardless enabled `Email as username` toggle. The bug was introduces in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/53ee95764e4bbfc0a075d7b91c872590fd376e46#diff-45cc3f2945c63a4269281feeea8d34243f7179eb761dba4d8627c34da87c648c which supposed to close https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16263 issue.

**Working version:** `20.0.5`
**Bug since:** `21.0.0`

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

If `Email as username` toggle is enabled changing the email address by a user at `/realms/{realm}/account/#/personal-info` will cause an automatically change of the username to the new email.

### Actual behavior

Changing the email address has no impact at username regardless enabled `Email as username` toggle. After changing the email, it becomes new value, the username stays equal previous email

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start last release: `docker run --rm -p 8888:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:21.1.1 start-dev`
2. Login with `admin:admin` at http://localhost:8888/admin/master/console
3. Create new realm `test`
4. Enable all toggles at http://localhost:8888/admin/master/console/#/test/realm-settings/login and expecially `Email as username`
5. Create new user with verified email address `test@8888.com` at http://localhost:8888/admin/master/console/#/test/users/add-user and set its password.
6. Verify the new user has same email and username at http://localhost:8888/admin/master/console/#/test/users
7. Impersonate the user
8. Go to http://localhost:8888/realms/test/account/#/personal-info and change its email to `new@8888.com`
9. Go to admin console http://localhost:8888/admin/master/console/#/test/users and observe the new email `new@8888.com` but old username `test@8888.com`
10. Login in private browser window to http://localhost:8888/realms/test/account/#/personal-info with `test@8888.com` and verify the `new@8888.com` email
11. Go to admin console http://localhost:8888/admin/master/console/#/test/users and observe the new email `new@8888.com` but old username `test@8888.com`


### Anything else?

Related issue: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/17692
commit_message:
Changing the email address has no impact at username regardless "Email as username" toggle
closes #20459

issue_title: Look ahead window setting in OTP policy is not accurate
issue_body:
### Description

The label and description of the `Look ahead window` setting in the OTP policy are not accurate. 

This setting is actually looking *around* given that the valid time window also takes into account the past. See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/utils/TimeBasedOTP.java#L80.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Better label and description of how the `Look ahead window` works. 

### Details

See description.
